indicator_text: The following types of lobbyists are included in the register: professional lobbyists/consultants, in-house company lobbyists, in-house organisation lobbyists, professional consultancies, law firms, think tanks, research institutions, public entities, government agencies/employees
section_or_category: Scope: The scope of what is included and excluded from the register
scoring_rule: Not specified (FOCAL is conceptual/unweighted; framework says "we have not added weights to our indicators")
source_quote: The following types of lobbyists are included in the register: professional lobbyists/consultants, in-house company lobbyists, in-house organisation lobbyists, professional consultancies, law firms (papers/text/Lacy_Nichols_2024__focal_scoping_review.txt:951)
notes: Single Table 3 row but enumerates 9 lobbyist types; FOCAL treats it as one indicator (Table 3 row count totals 50). Could be atomized into 9 sub-items.

indicator_text: There is no (or low) financial or time threshold to qualify/exempt lobbyists from registration
section_or_category: Scope: The scope of what is included and excluded from the register
scoring_rule: Not specified; paper itself flags this as subjective: "what is a 'low' financial or time threshold to qualify/exempt lobbyists from registration? This is a question we will consider in the next stage"
source_quote: There is no (or low) financial or time threshold to qualify/exempt lobbyists from registration (papers/text/Lacy_Nichols_2024__focal_scoping_review.txt:954)
notes: Paper acknowledges subjectivity; "low" is undefined in this conceptual iteration.

indicator_text: The following are included as targets of lobbying: legislative branches, executive branch officials, Ministers, Deputy Ministers, members of parliament, Director-Generals and senior officials, staff, administrative branch/bureaucracy
section_or_category: Scope: The scope of what is included and excluded from the register
source_quote: The following are included as targets of lobbying: legislative branches, executive branch officials, Ministers, Deputy Ministers, members of parliament, Director-Generals and senior officials, staff, administrative branch/bureaucracy (papers/text/Lacy_Nichols_2024__focal_scoping_review.txt:955)
notes: Single row enumerates 8 target types; treated as one indicator. Paper notes "some of these may be context dependent, for instance the target of lobbying may vary depending on the form of government (eg, a Westminster vs Presidential system)".
